Cross Defendant Angelique Brunner, Rachel Robasciotti'S Cross-Defendant Rachel J. Robasciottis Motion For An Order Compelling Arbitration Of The Negligence And Breach Of Fiduciary Causes Of Action Of The Amended Cross Complaint, And Staying The Balance Of The Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Aug-02-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, William Steven Weisberg of Weisberg & Miller (415-296-7070) appears as counsel for plaintiffs/cross-defendants/moving parties, Angelique Brunner and Rachel Robasciotti

Attorney, Jessica L. Chylik, Esq. (415-938-6552) appears as counsel for defendant/cross-complainant/opposing party, K. Wilson

Attorney, Patrick J. Connolly of Utrecht & Lenvin LLP (415-357-0600) appears as counsel for defendant/cross-complainant/opposing party, Debi Shargel

The cause is argued and submitted. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

Cross-Defendant Rachel Robasciotti's Motion For An Order Compelling Arbitration Of The Negligence And Breach Of Fiduciary Causes Of Action Of The Amended Cross-Complaint, And Staying The Balance Of The Cross-Complaint is in part GRANTED and in part DENIED. The motion to compel arbitration of the negligence and breach of fiduciary duty counts is GRANTED, but the motion to stay the non-arbitral parts of the cross-complaint is DENIED. Rather, the arbitration is STAYED under the third paragraph of CCP 1281.2(c) pending determination of the other issues between the parties in the case in this Court. The three-part requirement of 1281.2(c)'s first paragraph is in the conjunctive, and Wilson's opposition fails to demonstrate that "there is a possibility of conflicting rulings on a common issue of law or fact." However, determination of the non-arbitral issues between Wilson and Robasciotti in this court case "may make the arbitration unnecessary," so I "delay" my order to arbitrate as stated above. (See CCP 1281.2(c).) Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Laura Martinez, CSR #11332, Bus. Tel: 415-551-3745. (D302)

Plaintiff Angelique Brunner, Rachel Robasciotti'S Joint Notice Of Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings To Defendant Wilsons Amended Answer
Off Calendar May-10-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Elizabeth Hurwitz of Utrecht & Lenvin, LLP telephone number 415-357-0600 for Defendant Deborah Shargel.
No other appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. Plaintiffs Angelique Brunner and Rachel Robasciotti's Joint Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings To Defendant Wilson's Second Amended Answer is granted without leave to amend as to the fourth affirmative defense of statute of limitations, the sixth affirmative defense of attorney's fees and the seventh affirmative defense of good faith performance and denied as to all other affirmative defenses. Ms. Wilson acknowledges that her fourth, sixth and seventh affirmative defenses are not applicable. While a demurrer or motion for judgment on the pleadings as to affirmative defenses is permitted, unless it seeks to adjudicate an issue of law that is material to resolution of the case, which plaintiffs' motion does not, it serves no useful purpose. Most affirmative defenses are dropped prior to trial and are pled to preserve defendants' ability to defend the case based on information that comes out in discovery. If any further information is needed as to any affirmative defenses, discovery should be propounded. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported. (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion For Issuance Of Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Sep-16-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. KENNETH J. SPERANDIO, JR., ESQ. of Weisberg & Miller, Tel No. 415-296-7070 appearing for the Plaintiffs;
Atty. PAUL F. UTRECHT, ESQ. of Utrecht & Lenvin, LLP, Tel No. 415-357-0600 appearing for Defendanr Deborah Shargel.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. As to PLAINTIFFS ANGELIQUE BRUNNER AND RACHEL ROBASCIOTTI'S Motion For Issuance Of Preliminary Injunction, the motion is granted in part. Plaintiffs fail to demonstrate a basis to compel defendant to make additional payments. The parties' dispute is fundamentally about money and what type of offset should exist. That issue can be resolved at the end of the partition action. There is no evidence regarding the financial condition of the parties or whether irreparable harm exists. There is no showing that the property is about to go into default. The court enjoins both parties from encumbering the property without further order of the court. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)
